name: Fajita Nachos
recipeIngredient: 1 pound boned and skinned chicken breast halves (fat removed)
recipeInstructions:
For Salsa:
Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender and mix. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
Marinade:
Combine lime juice, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1/2 cup of the beer, garlic, coriander, cumin, and jalapeno in a freezer bag. Set aside 3 tablespoons and add chicken. Seal and rotate the bag to cover the chicken. Refrigerate for 2 hours or overnight, turning the bag occasionally.
Fajitas:
Combine the reserved marinade, beans, and remaining beer in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring and mashing the beans until slightly thickened. Season to taste with salt. In a frying pan, add the remaining oil over medium heat. Add onions and cook partially covered and stirring often until soft and golden brown. If made ahead, cover beans and onions separately, reheating when ready to use.
Remove chicken from the bag and drain any excess marinade. Arrange on a grill grate and place the lid on top of the grill. Cook the chicken until it is done, then remove and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Spread the beans onto a 10-inch circle in a large pizza pan. Top with onions, chicken, and cheese. Place the pan on the grill grate and cover with the lid. Cook until the cheese is melted. Top with guacamole and some of the salsa. Tuck some of the chips around the edges. Serve with remaining chips and salsa.
